title: torch-npu version resolution prevents unconstrained pip install
problem_description: When installing from requirements.txt without pinned versions, pip resolves torch to the latest version (2.11.0) which pulls incompatible torch-npu (2.2.0). This CANN 8.1.RC1 environment requires torch-npu 2.5.1 specifically, but pip's resolver does not know the CANN constraint and selects mismatched versions.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Before pip install, check the system-installed torch-npu version. 2. Pin torch to the matching major.minor version plus +cpu suffix (e.g., torch==2.5.1+cpu). 3. Install torch-npu with --no-deps to prevent version downgrade. 4. Install torchvision with matching +cpu wheel. 5. Verify all three import successfully before proceeding.
artifact_evidence: execution_journal.jsonl:Phase 2 dependency install and retry sequence

involved_code_files.role: Dependency specification — lists torch, torch-npu, torchvision without versions

reasoning: This is the most common failure mode in CUDA-to-NPU migrations. The version mismatch between upstream PyTorch and torch-npu is structural — torch-npu lags behind upstream torch releases and must be pinned to the CANN toolkit version. Any agent creating venvs for NPU projects must detect the system torch-npu version first and constrain all related packages accordingly.
